sanskrit:
आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषां रविरंशुमान्।
मरीचिर्मरुतामस्मि नक्षत्राणामहं शशी।।
transliteration: ādityānām ahaṁ viṣṇur jyotiṣāṁ ravir aṁśumān | marīcir marutām asmi nakṣatrāṇām ahaṁ śaśī ||
literal_meaning: Among the Ādityas I am Viṣṇu; among lights I am the radiant sun; among the Maruts I am Marīci; among the stars I am the moon.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names the brightest representatives of each cosmic class, showing that the Self is most vividly contacted through whatever most powerfully illuminates awareness in its domain.
psychological_state: Arjuna’s attention is being guided from scattered celestial objects to the singular awareness that lights them all—an invitation to shift from comparison to recognition of the one luminous Self.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media and feeling dwarfed by others’ brilliance, then realizing you can locate the same awareness in you that makes any mind glow.
anchor_text: Find the Self in whatever shines brightest to your mind.
